_meta.description: Pre-computed benchmark results displayed by the Streamlit Benchmarks tab.

honest_disclosure.what_is_real: All reference values (SCAPS PCE, measured PCE, Voc, Jsc, FF) are taken DIRECTLY from the cited peer-reviewed papers. SCAPS-reference values for D1-D6 are reproduced from Hossain et al., ACS Omega 7, 43210 (2022), Table 4 (CsPbI3-based devices). D7 reference values are reproduced from Chabri et al., J. Electron. Mater. 52, 2722 (2023) (Cs2SnI6-based device, room-temperature value). All cited DOIs have been verified via doi.org.
honest_disclosure.what_is_pre_computed: The 'Our PCE' column is generated by running the bundled drift-diffusion solver with input parameters reported in each paper (Hossain 2022 Tables 1-3 for CsPbI3 absorber, ETL, HTL, and interface defect parameters; Chabri 2023 Tables for Cs2SnI6 device parameters). To reproduce locally, run: `python scripts/run_benchmark.py` from the repository root.
honest_disclosure.what_is_an_estimate: The 'Our PCE' values shown here are the most recent local DD solver runs and may differ by ~0.1-0.3% absolute from a fresh run on different hardware due to mesh-discretization sensitivities. Re-run before publication.
honest_disclosure.how_to_regenerate: Run `python scripts/run_benchmark.py` for the SCAPS suite and `python scripts/run_experimental_benchmark.py` for the experimental suite. Both emit fresh JSON to artifacts/.

summary.note: All 7 devices have |dPCE| < 5%. Mean error 1.7%, well within the 1-3% intrinsic difference expected between independent implementations of the same drift-diffusion equations.

devices.reference_text: Hossain et al., ACS Omega 7, 43210 (2022), Table 4
devices.note: Hossain 2022 reports this as the lowest PCE among the six best CBTS-HTL devices (PCBM ETL has lower Jsc due to band alignment with CsPbI3). Our DD reproduces to 1.7% error.

devices.reference_text: Hossain et al., ACS Omega 7, 43210 (2022), Table 4
devices.note: BEST device in Hossain 2022 (17.90% PCE). Champion CsPbI3-based architecture. Our DD reproduces to 1.6% error.

devices.reference_text: Hossain et al., ACS Omega 7, 43210 (2022), Table 4
devices.note: ZnO ETL device (PCE 17.86%). Performance very close to TiO2 device due to similar band alignment with CsPbI3.

devices.reference_text: Hossain et al., ACS Omega 7, 43210 (2022), Table 4
devices.note: C60 ETL device (PCE 14.47%). Worst Hossain device due to unfavourable band alignment of C60 with CsPbI3. Slightly higher error (3.5%) reflects sensitivity to C60 mobility/affinity values.

devices.reference_text: Hossain et al., ACS Omega 7, 43210 (2022), Table 4
devices.note: IGZO ETL device (PCE 17.76%). Hossain reports highest carrier generation rate for this device (Figure 11c).

devices.reference_text: Hossain et al., ACS Omega 7, 43210 (2022), Table 4
devices.note: WS2 ETL device (PCE 17.82%). Hossain Figure 7 shows highest FF (85.65%) for this device at optimum thickness (1400-1600 nm absorber, 100-200 nm ETL).

devices.stack: ITO / CuI / Cs2SnI6 / ZnO / AZO / Ag
devices.reference_text: Chabri et al., J. Electron. Mater. 52, 2722 (2023)
devices.note: Lead-free Cs2SnI6 device (PCE 14.65% at 300 K, 16.77% at 400 K per Chabri 2023). Stack ITO/CuI/Cs2SnI6/ZnO/AZO/Ag is the final optimized configuration reported in the paper.

summary.interpretation_caveat: 1D DD solvers cannot capture 2D/3D effects, grain boundaries, pinholes, or area scaling. 15-20% errors against fabricated cells are expected and not a model failure.

devices.reference_text: Saliba et al., Energy Environ. Sci. 9, 1989 (2016)
devices.note: Stabilized 21.1% PCE reported. Triple-cation mixed-halide composition is approximated as MAPbI3 in our 1D model. Our DD over-predicts because it cannot capture grain-boundary recombination and area-scaling losses present in the fabricated cell.

devices.reference_text: Yang, Noh, Jeon, et al., Science 348, 1234 (2015) [NREL-certified]
devices.note: Certified 20.1% PCE via intramolecular DMSO/FAI exchange method. Foundational paper for high-quality FAPbI3 films.
